label: Google Cloud
description: Connect to Google Cloud Platform services including Cloud Storage, Compute Engine, Pub/Sub, and more using a service account.

form.title: Connect Google Cloud
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to interact with Google Cloud Platform services using a service account.

methods.label: In-Browser Connect
methods.description: Opens Google sign-in in your browser. No credentials required — just click Connect.

methods.label: Service Account JSON
methods.description: Paste a GCP service account key file (JSON) and your project ID. The simplest and most portable path — works with any GCP service Anton supports.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Service Account JSON key

A service account key lets Anton authenticate as a dedicated, permission-scoped identity on your GCP project. No browser sign-in required.

### Steps

1. Open the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts**.
3. Click **Create service account** (or select an existing one).
4. Give it a descriptive name like `anton-access` and click **Create and continue**.
5. On the **Grant access** step, assign the roles Anton needs. Common choices:
   - **Storage Object Viewer / Storage Object Admin** for Cloud Storage
   - **Compute Viewer** for Compute Engine
   - **Pub/Sub Subscriber** for Pub/Sub
   - Or **Project Viewer** for broad read-only access.
6. Click **Done**.
7. Open the service account, go to the **Keys** tab, and click **Add key → Create new key → JSON**.
8. The JSON key file downloads automatically. Open it and paste its entire contents into the **Service account JSON** field below.
9. Enter your **GCP Project ID** (visible in the top project picker bar, e.g. `my-project-123456`).

### Notes

- The JSON key is shown and downloadable only once. Store a backup in a secure vault.
- If the key is compromised, go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Keys** and delete it, then create a new one.
- Assign only the minimum roles needed for Anton's tasks — least privilege is best practice.
- For multi-region projects, confirm the resources Anton accesses are in the region you expect.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Paste the full contents of the JSON key file downloaded from IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Keys.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: Your Google Cloud project ID. Visible in the project picker at the top of the Cloud Console.

methods.label: Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Use your own Google Cloud OAuth client to authorize Anton via a browser consent screen. Useful when service account keys are disallowed by your org policy.
methods.how_to:
> **Pick the OAuth client type that matches your Anton shell:**
>
> - **Desktop app (Anton.app on macOS / Windows)** → in *Application type*, pick **Desktop app**. Google auto-allows loopback redirects, so no redirect-URI registration is needed.
> - **Web (browser-hosted Anton)** → pick **Web application** instead, then under *Authorized redirect URIs* add `http://127.0.0.1:26866/v1/connectors/oauth/callback` (substitute your server origin if you're behind a reverse proxy or running on a non-default port).
>
> The walkthrough below was written for the desktop path. If you're on web, read every "Desktop app" reference as "Web application" and make sure the redirect URI above is registered before clicking **Submit**.

## Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0 — BYOK)

This method lets Anton act as your user identity against GCP APIs. You must bring your own OAuth client from Google Cloud Console.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and pick your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable the APIs you need (e.g. **Cloud Storage API**, **Compute Engine API**).
3. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen**.
   - Choose **Internal** if your org uses Google Workspace; otherwise **External**.
   - Fill in app name and contact email. Add your email as a test user if External.
4. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
   - Application type: **Desktop app**.
   - Click **Create** and copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret**.
5. Paste both values into the fields below, then click **Submit**.
6. A browser window opens to Google's consent screen — approve the requested scopes.
7. Anton stores the resulting refresh token locally.

### Notes

- The `cloud-platform` scope grants broad access matching your user's IAM permissions. You cannot narrow it further at the OAuth level — restrict access via IAM roles on the project instead.
- If consent is needed again (e.g. after token expiry), Anton will prompt you to re-authorize.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID — copied from the same credential entry in Cloud Console.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: Your Google Cloud project ID. Visible in the project picker at the top of the Cloud Console.
